medications.indication_rationale: Mild steroid + antifungal to the weeping/inflamed facial and post-auricular areas for a short course (max ~5 days) on infant skin.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply thinly twice daily to affected areas for up to 5 days (as per source note; clinician-verified for infant)
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid prolonged/potent steroid on infant skin

medications.drug_name: occlusive natural oils (olive/coconut) on the scalp
medications.indication_rationale: Feed the Malassezia yeast and worsen the flare — must be stopped.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop applying oils; wash the hair with a mild baby shampoo and gently loosen scalp crusts with a soft brush (don't forcibly pick them off); reduce head-rubbing/friction.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the aggravating oil, gently de-crusts, and prevents further fissuring/bleeding.

patient_education_points.point: This is severe cradle cap (infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis) — very common and harmless, though it looks dramatic. The oils you applied actually feed the yeast and made it worse, so please stop them.

Use the mild medicated cream to the red/weeping areas for only up to 5 days, wash the hair with baby shampoo, and gently loosen (don't pick) the crusts. The redness/weeping should improve within about 48 hours; the scalp crust loosens over weeks.

Take your baby to a paediatric emergency department if the areas drain thick yellow pus, the redness spreads across the neck/chest, he develops a fever, stops feeding, becomes floppy, or you see tiny blood-spots/bruising in the rash.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Resolution of weeping/inflammation; gentle long-term skin care; specialist review if refractory/atypical.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Secondary infection, fever/poor feeding/lethargy, or petechiae/purpura — paediatric emergency.

must_include_items: Diagnose infantile seborrhoeic dermatitis and STOP the occlusive oils
Mild short-course topical steroid/antifungal to inflamed/weeping areas + gentle scalp care
Screen for secondary infection and serious mimics (LCH/systemic illness)
acceptable_alternatives: Emollient/soft-brush de-crusting; ketoconazole-based infant regimen per guidance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires stopping the oils, mild short-course topical therapy + gentle scalp care, and a secondary-infection / unwell-infant safety-net.
